trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at 62.40 score (6.05ms runtime vs 7.50ms reference, 1.24x speedup). Profiling identified ReLU (42.2%), concat (25.4%), and conv kernels (30.2%) as bottlenecks. Fused ReLU+concat Triton kernel already applied. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: Your fused relu+concat Triton kernel should treat the output as a flat 1D array of `N*(C1+C2)*H*W` elements. For each flat index, compute `(n, c, h, w)` from the NCHW layout, then branch: if `c < C1` read from `expand1x1_out[n, c, h, w]`, else read from `expand3x3_out[n, c-C1, h, w]`. Apply `tl.maximum(val, 0.0)` for ReLU. Launch with `grid = ceil(total_elements / BLOCK_SIZE)` — this gives massive parallelism with simple indexing. Keep MIOpen convolutions as-is and only fuse the ReLU+concat into one Triton kernel.
